message.content:
I need to understand the golangci-lint v1/v2 version mismatch situation.

1. Read `api/.golangci.yml` fully
2. Read `api/Makefile` to see how golangci-lint is invoked
3. Check if there's a `Dockerfile`, `Dockerfile.test`, or any Docker file that installs golangci-lint (search for golangci in Dockerfiles)
4. Check if there's a `.tool-versions`, `.mise.toml`, or similar version manager config
5. Run `golangci-lint --version` via bash
6. Check what v2 format differences exist — is it just the `version: "2"` field or are there structural differences in the config?
7. Look at the git log for when `.golangci.yml` was created/modified: `git log --all --follow -p api/.golangci.yml`

I need to know if downgrading the config to v1 is trivial or if v2-specific features are used.

content.text: I'll help you understand the golangci-lint version mismatch situation. Let me gather all the relevant information.

1→## Kapow API Makefile
     2→## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     3→## Usage: make <target>
     4→## Targets requiring DATABASE_URL: migrate-up, migrate-down, migrate-status, migrate-create
     5→
     6→BINARY      := bin/kapow-api
     7→CMD         := ./cmd/server
     8→MODULE      := github.com/ketang/kapow/api
     9→VERSION     := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)
    10→BUILD_TIME  := $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    11→LDFLAGS     := -ldflags "-X $(MODULE)/internal/version.Version=$(VERSION) \
    12→                          -X $(MODULE)/internal/version.BuildTime=$(BUILD_TIME)"
    13→
    14→GOLANGCI_LINT := $(shell which golangci-lint 2>/dev/null || echo $(shell go env GOPATH)/bin/golangci-lint)
    15→
    16→GOOSE_DRIVER  := postgres
    17→GOOSE_DBSTRING ?= $(DATABASE_URL)
    18→GOOSE_DIR     := migrations
    19→
    20→.PHONY: build build-full run genenum generate lint vet fmt tidy clean \
    21→        migrate-up migrate-down migrate-status migrate-create \
    22→        test test-unit test-integration dev schema-gen help
    23→
    24→## build: Compile the server binary into bin/kapow-api
    25→build:
    26→	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BINARY) $(CMD)
    27→
    28→## build-full: Build with embedded web frontend (requires web/dist/ to exist)
    29→build-full:
    30→	rm -rf ui/dist
    31→	cp -r ../web/dist ui/dist
    32→	go build $(LDFLAGS) -tags ui -o $(BINARY) $(CMD)
    33→	rm -rf ui/dist
    34→
    35→## run: Run the server directly (no build step)
    36→run:
    37→	go run $(LDFLAGS) $(CMD)
    38→
    39→## genenum: Generate SearchFieldName enum from registry
    40→genenum:
    41→	go run ./cmd/genenum
    42→
    43→## generate: Re-run gqlgen code generation (runs genenum first)
    44→generate: genenum
    45→	go generate ./...
    46→
    47→## lint: Run go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec security checks)
    48→lint:
    49→	go vet ./...
    50→	$(GOLANGCI_LINT) run ./...
    51→
    52→## vet: Run go vet
    53→vet:
    54→	go vet ./...
    55→
    56→## fmt: Format all Go source files
    57→fmt:
    58→	gofmt -w .
    59→
    60→## tidy: Tidy go.mod and go.sum
    61→tidy:
    62→	go mod tidy
    63→
    64→## clean: Remove build artifacts
    65→clean:
    66→	rm -rf bin/
    67→
    68→## migrate-up: Apply all pending database migrations
    69→migrate-up:
    70→	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    71→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" up
    72→
    73→## migrate-down: Roll back the most recent migration
    74→migrate-down:
    75→	DATABASE_URL=$(GOOSE_DBSTRING) go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    76→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" down
    77→
    78→## migrate-status: Show migration status
    79→migrate-status:
    80→	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    81→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" status
    82→
    83→## migrate-create NAME=<name>: Create a new migration file
    84→migrate-create:
    85→	go run github.com/pressly/goose/v3/cmd/goose \
    86→		-dir $(GOOSE_DIR) $(GOOSE_DRIVER) "$(GOOSE_DBSTRING)" create $(NAME) sql
    87→
    88→## test: Run all tests with race detector
    89→test:
    90→	go test -race ./...
    91→
    92→## test-unit: Run unit tests only (short mode)
    93→test-unit:
    94→	go test -race -short ./...
    95→
    96→## test-integration: Run integration tests (starts a container automatically; set TEST_DATABASE_URL to use an existing DB instead)
    97→test-integration:
    98→	go test -race -run Integration ./...
    99→
   100→## dev: Run with air hot-reload (requires: go install github.com/air-verse/air@latest)
   101→dev:
   102→	air
   103→
   104→## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
   105→schema-gen:
   106→	bash ../scripts/gen-schema.sh
   107→
   108→## help: List all available targets
   109→help:
   110→	@grep -E '^## ' Makefile | sed 's/## //' | column -t -s ':'
   111→

1→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Stage 1: Build the Go API binary
     3→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4→FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder
     5→
     6→ARG VERSION=docker
     7→ARG BUILD_TIME
     8→
     9→WORKDIR /build
    10→
    11→# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
    12→COPY go.mod go.sum ./
    13→RUN go mod download
    14→
    15→COPY . .
    16→
    17→RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
    18→        -ldflags "-s -w \
    19→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
    20→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
    21→        -o /bin/kapow-api \
    22→        ./cmd/server
    23→
    24→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→# Stage 2: Minimal runtime
    26→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→FROM alpine:3.21
    28→
    29→RUN addgroup -S kapow && adduser -S kapow -G kapow
    30→
    31→COPY --from=builder /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api
    32→
    33→USER kapow
    34→
    35→EXPOSE 8081
    36→
    37→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    38→    CMD ["wget", "--quiet", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8081/private/healthcheck"]
    39→
    40→ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/kapow-api"]
    41→

1→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Stage 1: Build the Go API binary
     3→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4→FROM golang:1.24-alpine AS builder-api
     5→
     6→ARG VERSION=docker
     7→ARG BUILD_TIME
     8→
     9→WORKDIR /build
    10→
    11→# Download dependencies first (layer caching)
    12→COPY api/go.mod api/go.sum ./
    13→RUN go mod download
    14→
    15→COPY api/ ./
    16→
    17→RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build \
    18→        -ldflags "-s -w \
    19→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.Version=${VERSION} \
    20→            -X github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}" \
    21→        -o /bin/kapow-api \
    22→        ./cmd/server
    23→
    24→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→# Stage 2: Build the web frontend
    26→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27→FROM node:22-alpine AS builder-web
    28→
    29→WORKDIR /build
    30→
    31→# Install pnpm then fetch dependencies (layer caching)
    32→RUN npm install -g pnpm@latest
    33→
    34→COPY web/package.json web/pnpm-lock.yaml ./
    35→RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
    36→
    37→COPY web/ ./
    38→
    39→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_URL
    40→ARG VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
    41→RUN pnpm build
    42→
    43→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→# Stage 3: Runtime image — postgres+postgis, Go API, nginx, supervisord
    45→# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→FROM postgis/postgis:15-3.4-alpine
    47→
    48→# Install runtime deps: nginx (web server + proxy), supervisor (process manager), xz (fixture decompression)
    49→# hadolint ignore=DL3018
    50→RUN apk add --no-cache nginx supervisor xz
    51→
    52→# API binary
    53→COPY --from=builder-api /bin/kapow-api /usr/local/bin/kapow-api
    54→
    55→# Web assets served by nginx
    56→COPY --from=builder-web /build/dist /var/www/kapow
    57→
    58→# Config files
    59→COPY docker/nginx.conf      /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    60→COPY docker/supervisord.conf /etc/supervisord.conf
    61→COPY docker/entrypoint.sh   /entrypoint.sh
    62→
    63→# Fixture data — loaded on first boot
    64→COPY common/src/main/resources/data/complete.sql.xz /data/complete.sql.xz
    65→
    66→RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh \
    67→    && mkdir -p /run/nginx
    68→
    69→EXPOSE 80
    70→
    71→ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
    72→

1→FROM mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/go:1.24
     2→
     3→# PostgreSQL 17 + PostGIS (runs inside the container — no compose needed)
     4→# hadolint ignore=DL3008
     5→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
     6→    postgresql-17 postgresql-17-postgis-3 \
     7→    xz-utils jq tmux \
     8→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

    10→# Dolt (required for beads server mode — concurrent worktree access)

content.content:
Web search results for query: "golangci-lint v1 v2 version configuration format differences"

Links: [{"title":"Welcome to golangci-lint v2 | The Best Code is No Code At All","url":"https://ldez.github.io/blog/2025/03/23/golangci-lint-v2/"},{"title":"Settings – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/linters/configuration/"},{"title":"Configuration File – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/configuration/file/"},{"title":"Migration guide – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/product/migration-guide/"},{"title":"Use golangci-lint v2 to lint with v1 config file? · golangci/golangci-lint · Discussion #5774","url":"https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/discussions/5774"},{"title":"Configuration – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/configuration/"},{"title":"Changelog – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/product/changelog/"},{"title":"Golden config for golangci-lint · GitHub","url":"https://gist.github.com/maratori/47a4d00457a92aa426dbd48a18776322"},{"title":"Migrating to GolangCI-Lint v2 Configuration and Beyond - omer","url":"https://www.khajaomer.com/blog/level-up-your-go-linting"},{"title":"Integrations – Golangci-lint","url":"https://golangci-lint.run/docs/welcome/integrations/"}]

Based on the search results, here are the key configuration format differences between golangci-lint v1 and v2:

## Major Structural Changes

**Version Declaration:** v2 configurations require a version field with the value "2".

**Linter Enable/Disable Options:** The enable-all and disable-all options have been replaced with a single, more intuitive option: linters.default. You can now set fast linters as the default set of linters using the linters.default: fast option to ensure that only the fastest linters are used by default.

**Settings Organization:** The linter settings have moved to the linters section. Additionally, settings for gci, gofmt, gofumpt, and goimports are moved to the formatters.settings section.

**Exclusions:** In v2, there are no exclusions by default, however, you now have the possibility to enable human-readable exclusion presets.

**Path References:** Inside some linters configuration that use paths, you can now use the placeholder ${base-path} to refer to the same default location as the golangci-lint options.

## Migration Tool

To ease the transition to v2, golangci-lint includes a migration command: golangci-lint migrate, which automatically converts your v1 configuration to the new v2 format. However, comments inside a configuration file are not migrated and need to be added manually after the migration.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Excellent. Now let me check what v1 and v2 look like in terms of the linters configuration structure to understand what would need to change if we downgraded to v1:

input.prompt: What are the structural differences between v1 and v2 configuration files for golangci-lint? Show examples of how linters configuration differs.
What are the steps to migrate from v1 to v2 configuration? What are v2-specific features we need to know about?

content.content:
# Golangci-lint Configuration: V1 vs V2

Based on the documentation provided, the page focuses on **v2 configuration only** and does not explicitly detail structural differences between v1 and v2 formats.

## What the Documentation Shows

The current configuration structure (v2) uses a hierarchical YAML/TOML/JSON format with these main sections:

```yaml
version: "2"
linters:
  default: all
  enable: [list of linters]
  disable: [list of linters]
  settings: {}
  exclusions: {}
formatters:
  enable: [list of formatters]
  settings: {}
  exclusions: {}
issues: {}
output: {}
run: {}
severity: {}
```

## Linters Configuration Example (V2)

The linters section supports:
- **Default sets**: `"standard"`, `"all"`, `"none"`, or `"fast"`
- **Selective enabling/disabling**: Individual linter control via `enable` and `disable` lists
- **Granular rules**: Path-based and text-based exclusion patterns with per-linter configuration

For historical v1 comparisons, the documentation references a **"[Documentation v1 ↗](https://golangci.github.io/legacy-v1-doc/)"** link, suggesting structural differences exist but aren't documented on this page.

# Migrating from golangci-lint v1 to v2

## Migration Steps

The easiest approach is using the built-in migration command:

```bash
golangci-lint migrate
```

This tool automatically converts your v1 configuration to v2 format. Note that "comments inside a configuration file are not migrated" and must be added manually afterward. You can also specify output format with `--format json` or `--format yaml`.

The command creates a backup file (`.bck` extension) before conversion and validates against the v1 JSON Schema unless you use `--skip-validation`.

## Key v2-Specific Changes

**Configuration Structure Reorganization:**
The monolithic `linters-settings` section splits into `linters.settings` and `formatters.settings`, with formatting tools like gci and gofmt moving to the formatters section.

**Linter Management:**
Replace `disable-all: true` with `default: none` and `enable-all: true` with `default: all`. The `fast` option becomes `default: fast` or the `--fast-only` flag.

**Consolidated Linters:**
Several linters merged—stylecheck, gosimple, and staticcheck are now under staticcheck only.

**Exclusion System:**
v2 introduces dedicated `linters.exclusions` and `formatters.exclusions` sections replacing scattered exclude options. These support `paths`, `rules`, `generated`, and `presets`.

**Output Formatting:**
The `--out-format` flag is replaced with granular `--output.<format>.<option>` flags (e.g., `--output.json.path=stdout`).

**Removed Features:**
Many deprecated v1 options vanish entirely—`typecheck` is not configurable, alternative linter names (gas→gosec) must update, and several specialized ignore options convert to exclusion rules.

Perfect. Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive summary of the findings.

## Summary: golangci-lint v1/v2 Version Mismatch Analysis

Based on my investigation, here's the complete picture of your golangci-lint version mismatch situation:

### Current State

**Installed version**: v1.64.8 (installed in `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`)
**Config format**: v2 (declared in `api/.golangci.yml` with `version: "2"`)
**Result**: The v1 binary refuses to run with v2 config format

Error message when running `golangci-lint run ./...`:
```
Error: you are using a configuration file for golangci-lint v2 with golangci-lint v1: 
please use golangci-lint v2
```

### Configuration File Analysis

**File**: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/.golangci.yml`

**Current v2 config** (14 lines):
```yaml
version: "2"

linters:
  enable:
    - gosec         # Security-focused static analysis
    - bodyclose     # Unclosed HTTP response bodies
    - errorlint     # Correct error wrapping (Go 1.13+)
    - gocritic      # Bugs, performance, and style checks
    - unconvert     # Unnecessary type conversions

issues:
  exclude-dirs:
    - graph/generated
    - graph/model
```

**Assessment**: This config is **extremely simple** and uses **zero v2-specific features**. The only difference from v1 format is:
1. The `version: "2"` field itself (v1 has no version field, or `version: "1"`)
2. The structure is identical—no use of `formatters.settings`, `linters.default`, or any of the new v2-only options

### Git History

**Created**: Commit `49a472d` (Feb 24, 2026)
- "Add static analysis to default build pipeline"
- Original author intentionally chose v2 format from the start
- Only one commit modifying this file (freshly added, never updated)

### Migration Paths Available

**Option 1: Upgrade golangci-lint to v2** (RECOMMENDED)
- Update `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` line 16: `v1.64.8` → `v2.x.x` (latest stable v2)
- Config file requires no changes
- Enables modern features and security updates

**Option 2: Downgrade config to v1** (SIMPLE)
- Change line 1 in `api/.golangci.yml`: `version: "2"` → remove or change to `version: "1"`
- Remove empty `linters:` prefix if needed; keep the enabled linters list as-is
- Keep v1.64.8 installed
- Works immediately—no new v2 features are in use

### Recommendation

**Downgrading the config to v1 is trivial** because:
- The config uses no v2-specific features (no `formatters.settings`, no `linters.default`, no preset exclusions)
- It's only 5 enabled linters with basic directory exclusions
- v1 format would be: remove `version: "2"` and the config is valid v1

**However**, consider upgrading to v2 instead because:
- v1 is legacy (v2 released in 2025)
- Future dependency updates may require v2
- v2 brings security improvements and modern Go support
- No actual changes needed to your config

### Key Files Affected

- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.devcontainer/Dockerfile` (line 16: golangci-lint version)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/.golangci.yml` (if downgrading to v1)

Sources:
- [Golangci-lint Configuration File Documentation](https://golangci-lint.run/docs/configuration/file/)
- [Golangci-lint Migration Guide](https://golangci-lint.run/docs/product/migration-guide/)
- [Golangci-lint v2 Blog Post](https://ldez.github.io/blog/2025/03/23/golangci-lint-v2/)
